title_normalized: Engineering Manager, GenAI Platform Development Experience

architecture.latency_reliability: SLOs, dashboards, alerts, runbooks, staged rollouts/rollbacks, on-call rotation, incident response

success_metrics: company-wide productivity gains from GenAI tools
adoption and understanding of usage patterns
ship value fast without breaking trust
implied_pain_points: build and operate the core GenAI platform
increase adoption of internal GenAI tools
secure and compliant AI delivery
improve platform analytics and usage insights
maintain reliability and cost control
hire and develop the team

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 8+ years software engineering and 2+ years engineering manager

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A top CV should show hands-on engineering leadership on an AI platform, not just people management. The strongest evidence would be an AI workflow or platform launch with real users, plus clear ownership of reliability, evaluation, guardrails, and operating cadence.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: formal engineering manager tenure unless he has managed directly
AWS/GCP or Kubernetes ownership unless already documented
large-scale QPS or enterprise-user scale unless quantified
security/compliance ownership in regulated enterprise AI unless explicitly shown
P&L or budget ownership
building a production GenAI platform with no evidence of evaluation or guardrails
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Technical Lead at Seven.One Entertainment Group: closest fit for hands-on leadership, architecture reviews, and cross-functional execution
Commander-4: strongest match for the GenAI platform surface area because it includes RAG, semantic caching, evaluation, guardrails, and enterprise adoption
Lantern: relevant for routing, multi-provider control, and reliability/cost tradeoffs in an AI platform

implied_pain_points: prioritize and maintain a GenAI product backlog
translate strategy into actionable backlog items
coordinate engineering, data science, and UX delivery
manage product lifecycle from ideation to continuous improvement
drive adoption and communicate progress to stakeholders

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 3-5 years Product Owner or Product Manager

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs clear product ownership signals, not only engineering delivery. The best evidence would be examples of prioritizing a backlog, translating business needs into user stories, coordinating launches, and showing measurable adoption or business impact for an AI product.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: formal product manager ownership of backlog and roadmap unless explicitly demonstrated
product marketing or go-to-market ownership unless shown
hands-on ML framework work if the experience is only platform engineering
direct customer-facing product leadership at company-event level
using Jira/Confluence as a PM if not documented
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4: closest AI-product proxy because it shows an internal product with real users, adoption concerns, and operational follow-through
Technical Lead at Seven.One Entertainment Group: useful for cross-functional delivery and turning requirements into shipped systems
Lantern: relevant for platform thinking and product tradeoffs, but it is weaker than Commander-4 for explicit product-management evidence

implied_pain_points: launch and scale AI products
turn data and user feedback into product decisions
create playbooks and training materials for internal and client use
align engineering, design, and leadership around priorities
provide executive-ready updates and product messaging

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: The CV should prove 0→1 execution on an AI or software product, plus enough technical depth to prototype with engineers. It should also show that the candidate can turn user feedback into prioritized decisions, produce enablement material, and communicate clearly to leadership.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: formal startup founder experience unless explicitly shown
product marketing ownership unless supported by concrete examples
SQL-heavy analytics ownership unless he has done it before
executive-level product influence unless there is proof of that scope
client-facing enablement work such as demos and screencasts unless already documented
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4: best match because it is a real AI product with users, launch decisions, adoption, and platform tradeoffs
Lantern: useful for productizing infrastructure-style AI capabilities such as routing and reliability
Technical Lead at Seven.One Entertainment Group: supports the cross-functional execution and technical prototyping side of the role

implied_pain_points: turn user feedback and data into product decisions
launch and scale AI products in an ambiguous startup environment
create enablement assets such as playbooks, training, and dashboards
align priorities with leadership while balancing hands-on execution

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: The CV needs to show product-minded AI delivery, not just engineering depth: 0 to 1 launches, user adoption, and clear conversion of customer feedback into roadmap decisions. It should also show cross-functional work with design and leadership, plus tangible outcomes from prototypes, playbooks, and analytics.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: formal product management ownership of the full roadmap and go-to-market
deep product marketing leadership across multiple launches
hands-on ownership of sales and customer-success operations
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 is the strongest match if framed as an AI product launch with adoption metrics, because it shows an enterprise AI workflow platform used by 2000 users.
Lantern maps well because it shows AI platform thinking, routing, and reliability decisions rather than only feature delivery.
Technical Lead experience at Seven.One supports cross-functional execution, but the CV should frame it as product ownership and user impact, not just system design.

title_normalized: Senior Engineering Manager, AI Applications Platform
implied_pain_points: abstract complex AI technologies into reusable platform capabilities
help product teams ship AI features quickly and safely
provide enterprise-ready access to LLMs with governance and privacy built in
improve developer experience and adoption across multiple product teams

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 5+ years of experience in technical leadership managing software engineering teams

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: The CV needs to prove that the candidate can turn complex AI infrastructure into reusable platform capabilities that multiple teams adopt. It should also show governance, privacy, and reliability decisions in production, not just experimentation.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: running SRE, security operations, or database operations end to end
formal GDPR or CCPA compliance program ownership at enterprise scale
managing large engineering teams with significant direct-report depth
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 is the best match because it already shows reusable AI platform capabilities, RAG, evaluation, guardrails, and adoption by 2000 users.
Lantern maps strongly to LLM access, multi-provider routing, and reliability patterns such as circuit breaking.
Prior CQRS/event-sourcing and microservices work supports the architectural abstraction and platform thinking this role needs.

architecture.latency_reliability: scalability, performance, security, maintainability, frequent reliable deployments

implied_pain_points: lead a distributed engineering organization across time zones and employment models
improve platform scalability and reliability for a production-grade software system
build out data warehouse and BI capabilities
reduce operational toil and cycle time through process and tooling improvements

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: The CV should show executive-level technical leadership over complex distributed systems, with evidence of reliability, scale, and cross-functional execution. It also needs to demonstrate people leadership, delivery discipline, and ownership of data-platform or infrastructure modernization work.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: VP-level org ownership with direct management of multiple directors
owning IT, cybersecurity, and infrastructure operations end to end
large-scale budget or P&L responsibility
best_candidate_experience_mapping: The Technical Lead role at Seven.One is the best anchor because it shows engineering leadership on an enterprise AI platform and cross-functional delivery.
Commander-4 and Lantern provide evidence of platform architecture, reliability thinking, and production AI systems.
Prior CQRS/event-sourcing and microservices/RabbitMQ work maps to the distributed platform and operational rigor this role needs.

implied_pain_points: needs someone to turn AI product ideas into shipped outcomes from concept to launch
needs roadmap prioritization based on data and user feedback
needs stronger adoption through product marketing, onboarding, and playbooks
needs cross-functional alignment across engineering, design, leadership, and clients

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV should show ownership of an AI or software product from concept to launch, with evidence of prioritization, user adoption, and measurable outcomes. It should also show comfort working directly with engineers and designers, plus using data to make product decisions.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Do not claim formal product management ownership unless it is directly evidenced.
Do not claim pricing, GTM, or product marketing ownership without explicit proof.
Do not claim hands-on use of Canva, HubSpot, Notion, Airtable, or Asana unless he has actually used them.
Do not claim people management, hiring, or budget ownership.
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 is the closest match because it is an enterprise AI workflow platform with 2,000 users, 42 plugins, RAG, semantic caching, evaluation, and guardrails; that is strong evidence of AI product delivery and adoption.
Lantern also helps because it shows AI platform thinking around multi-provider routing and reliability, which supports the 'prototype and deploy' part of the role.
His prior backend and microservices work is weaker for this role, but it can support the execution and cross-functional credibility parts of the story.

implied_pain_points: needs someone to turn AI product ideas into shipped outcomes from concept to launch
needs roadmap prioritization based on data and user feedback
needs stronger adoption through product marketing, onboarding, and playbooks
needs cross-functional alignment across engineering, design, leadership, and clients

implied_pain_points: turn AI product ideas into launched products
prioritize roadmaps from user feedback, research, and metrics
drive user adoption with playbooks, training, and product marketing assets
align engineering, design, leadership, and executive stakeholders
own outcomes across quality, delivery, and user impact

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs to show you have taken AI capabilities from concept to shipped product, not just built internal tooling. It should prove you can prioritize roadmaps, work across engineering and design, and translate user feedback and metrics into decisions, playbooks, and adoption gains.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: formal product management ownership of roadmaps and product marketing without clear PM evidence
full P&L or pricing ownership
direct hiring or performance management of a product organization
owning go-to-market strategy or demand generation campaigns as the primary owner
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 is the strongest match because it shows an enterprise AI product shipped to 2,000 users with RAG, semantic caching, evaluation, and guardrails, which maps well to launch, adoption, and measurable value.
Lantern maps to prototyping and productizing AI capabilities with multi-provider routing and circuit-breaking, which supports the reliability and execution side of the role.
Your technical lead and prior distributed-systems work help demonstrate cross-functional execution and the ability to communicate tradeoffs to engineering and leadership.

improve adoption through messaging, demos, and training materials

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV should show that the candidate has led 0->1 AI or software products, worked cross-functionally with engineering/design/leadership, and used data plus customer feedback to make product decisions. It should also show concrete output from product enablement work such as playbooks, training materials, demos, or dashboards, plus clear examples of shipping outcomes with user impact.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Formal product manager ownership if the work was actually in engineering leadership rather than PM
Direct P&L ownership
Ownership of product marketing end-to-end unless explicitly done
People management or hiring responsibility
Specific hands-on use of Canva, Asana, Airtable, or HubSpot unless explicitly true
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps best because it is a 0->1 enterprise AI workflow platform with 42 plugins, 2000 users, RAG, evaluation, and guardrails, which is close to shipping and iterating AI product capabilities with measurable adoption.
Lantern maps well because multi-provider routing and circuit breaker design show product-enabling platform thinking, especially around reliability and operational readiness.
The Seven.One technical lead role supports the cross-functional engineering leadership and stakeholder collaboration angle needed for an AI product manager.

implied_pain_points: Traditional Search and AI enabled Search need one team-owned solution
the team needs cost-effective practices while scaling search infrastructure
the manager must improve team execution, hiring, and performance under pressure

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV should show you can lead engineers while staying close enough to the code to shape design and integration decisions. The best proof is a shipped AI/search platform with distributed-systems depth, plus concrete examples of hiring, performance management, and cost-aware execution.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Owning traditional search relevance or retrieval at large scale if you only built adjacent AI workflows
Managing a large org with budget or P&L responsibility
Deep C#-first engineering leadership if that is not actually your primary background
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps strongly because it combines RAG, evaluation, guardrails, plugins, and 2,000 users in an enterprise AI workflow platform
Lantern maps strongly because multi-provider routing and circuit breaking mirror the reliability and cost-control side of AI infrastructure
Technical Lead experience at Seven.One supports the hands-on leadership, architecture, and cross-team decision-making expected here

title_normalized: Engineering Manager, AI/ML Infrastructure & GenAI Platforms

architecture.scale_signals: scale across use cases and products

success_metrics: deliver the core infrastructure powering LLM-based applications
safe, observable, cost-efficient access to multiple foundation models
improve software delivery and service reliability
ship architecturally sound software at a regular cadence
implied_pain_points: the company needs a secure, cost-optimized LLM platform for product teams
the team needs a reliable internal LLM proxy and orchestration layer
AI infrastructure must scale across multiple use cases without losing observability

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: The CV should prove you can own LLM infrastructure end to end: design, rollout, reliability, observability, and cost control. It should show platform ownership with concrete outcomes around safe model access, evaluation, and production AI tooling at scale.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Deep AWS, Kubernetes, or Kafka ownership if you have not operated that stack in production
Leading a dedicated ML or AI platform organization at multi-team scale
Hands-on model training or fine-tuning work if your background is primarily platform and integration
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Lantern is the closest match because it is already an LLM gateway with multi-provider routing and circuit-breaker behavior
Commander-4 matches the platform and governance side through RAG, evaluation, semantic caching, guardrails, and enterprise deployment
Technical leadership at Seven.One supports the cross-functional ownership style Zendesk wants

implied_pain_points: the team needs to capture AI developer-tool growth through fast experimentation
native integrations and agentic builders are needed to improve activation and adoption
architecture must balance velocity with maintainability across the product surface

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV should prove you can ship developer-facing AI tooling, keep product velocity high, and still make durable architecture decisions. The strongest evidence is a product or platform that drove adoption through integrations, plus examples of experimentation, metrics-driven iteration, and cross-functional collaboration.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Owning growth experimentation and funnel optimization if you have not run A/B tests or owned activation metrics
Deep MCP-specific product experience if you have not actually built MCP servers or MCP apps
Full-stack leadership in TypeScript, React, and Next.js if your recent work has been mostly backend or platform focused
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 is the closest analog to developer tooling because it is an AI workflow platform with plugins, integrations, and user adoption
Lantern maps to the platform integration and routing side of the role
Prior microservices and real-time systems work supports the architecture and delivery expectations, even if the growth-experimentation part is weaker
